Scenario.overview: Communications Simple This scenario outlines a basic cyber attack targeting a communication sector entity using phishing techniques to gain initial access, executing malicious files to compromise the system, and causing impact through data manipulation.
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Internet-facing assets, File System, OS Process
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: Delivery Method, Reg.exe Utility, Data Manipulation

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.kev: CVE-2023-28432: Information Disclosure in MinIO Cluster Deployment
stage.description: An attacker sends a phishing email with a malicious attachment or link to trick employees into downloading and opening it, providing initial access to the network.

stage.name: Execution
stage.kev: CVE-2020-9054: Command Injection Vulnerability in Zyxel NAS Devices
stage.capec: CAPEC-635: Alternative Execution Due to Deceptive Filenames
stage.description: The victim opens the malicious attachment/link, triggering the execution of malware on their machine.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: The attacker manipulates communications data by altering settings or injecting false information into the systems, causing disruption and potential data corruption.

Scenario.summary: In this simple attack scenario, a basic-level attacker uses phishing to gain entry into a communication sector company, followed by executing malicious files to compromise the system. Finally, they cause impact by manipulating data, potentially disrupting services or compromising integrity.
